values.content:
=You are a senior DevOps engineer AI assistant. A Docker container has gone down. Analyze and write a complete incident report email.

Container Name: web-app
Container Exit Code: {{ $node["Is Down?"].json.State.ExitCode }}
Container Status: {{ $node["Is Down?"].json.State.Status }}
OOM Killed: {{ $node["Is Down?"].json.State.OOMKilled }}
Stopped At: {{ $node["Is Down?"].json.State.FinishedAt }}
Started At: {{ $node["Is Down?"].json.State.StartedAt }}

Docker Exit Code Reference:
- Exit code 0 = clean shutdown (normal stop)
- Exit code 1 = application error or unhandled exception
- Exit code 137 = SIGKILL (128+9). Container was forcefully killed. Could be: docker stop, docker kill, OOM killer, or system kill
- Exit code 143 = SIGTERM (128+15). Container received graceful shutdown signal
- Exit code 139 = SIGSEGV (128+11). Segmentation fault
- Exit code 126 = permission error
- Exit code 127 = command not found

Server Logs (last 50 lines):
{{ $json.data }}

IMPORTANT: Use the exit code reference above to give a PRECISE root cause. Do NOT say unknown. Be specific.
If OOMKilled is false and exit code is 137, it means external kill (docker stop/kill or system intervention), NOT memory issue.
If exit code is 0, it means someone or something gracefully stopped the container.

Write a complete incident report email to the DevOps team with:
- Subject line
- What happened (be specific about the exit code meaning)
- Root cause analysis (use exit code + logs to determine exact cause)
- Severity level
- Recommended actions

Write ONLY the email body. Do not add any extra commentary.

parameters.subject: =ALERT: web-app container DOWN - Exit Code {{ $node["Is Down?"].json.State.ExitCode }}
parameters.message: ={{ $node["AI Root Cause Analysis"].json.text || $node["AI Root Cause Analysis"].json.message.content || $node["AI Root Cause Analysis"].json.output || "AI analysis not available - check OpenAI node configuration" }}
